text:
Once upon a time in a little village far, far away, there lived a cat named Whiskers. Whiskers was unusual. He liked poetry. He had a little black book filled with verses he’d compose.

One moonlit night, while everybody was asleep, Whiskers composed a poem so beautiful the stars seemed to shine brighter. He called it “The Night Owls.”

“The night owls love your feathers,
Their eyes so big and bright.”

He couldn’t wait to read it out loud.

The next morning, Owlsworth came fluttering in.

“Ah! Your verses! Truly poetic!”

“Just one more night owl.”

“Oh, you loves poetry?”

“Every day.”

“You must be wonderful.”

He read some more. Owlsworth felt so moved.

“Fledgling warbler.”

“My oh my, that’s soft.”

“Just my favorite song.”

They stayed close that evening.

When dawn arrived once more, Whiskers sadly folded his book.

“You
